body:
## Summary

Fixes #221 — stops faking translation for common nouns. The Rust
pipeline and the browser demo now translate any reachable noun through
the real `source → formalize → meaning → deformalize → target` path
using only Wikipedia / Wikidata / Wiktionary data. No LLM in the loop,
no pre-extracted dictionary in the repo.

**This revision (2026-05-22) follows the PR review feedback:** the
previous 128-entry offline dictionary is gone. The only legal cached
artefact is now a **verbatim API response body** stored as
**human-readable raw text** inside indented Links Notation files under
`data/seed/api-cache/`, capped at 128 records per semantic bucket and
1500 lines per file. Bodies use Links Notation's doubled-quote escape
(one literal `"` is written as two consecutive `"`); no other character
is escaped, so reviewers can read the raw API JSON directly. Anything
that cannot be served from the bundle falls through to the on-disk
accelerator (gitignored) and then to live HTTP. The browser worker has
its own live fallback so it never needs a bundled dictionary.

Full investigation lives in `docs/case-studies/issue-221/`
(timeline through 2026-05-22, eleven verbatim requirements, five root
causes, fixes, before/after table, verification commands, upstream
defect notes, and online research on Wiktionary CORS / Wikidata SPARQL
polysemy).

## Changes

### Raw API-response seed bundle (replaces the dictionary)
- `data/seed/api-cache/*.lino` — verbatim Wikidata and Wiktionary API
  response bodies, indented Links Notation with **plain-text** payloads
  (chunks split at ≤200 chars; values use Links Notation's
  doubled-quote escape — one literal `"` becomes `""`). Bundle is
  capped at 128 records per bucket (entities, properties, search,
  sparql, pages-per-language) and every file stays under 1500 lines;
  oversize bodies split into `<bucket>-partN.lino` parts that re-join
  at load by URL.
- `build.rs` enumerates the `.lino` files at compile time and emits
  `seed_bundle_files.rs` into `OUT_DIR`; `src/translation/cache.rs`
  picks it up via `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), ...))` so adding
  a new part file is automatic — no per-file `include_str!` edit.
- `examples/refresh_translation_cache.rs` drives the full pipeline
  against a curated 128-noun seed list, populates the on-disk
  accelerator, then rebundles into the committed `.lino` files
  (splitting oversize records, cleaning stale parts).
- `experiments/convert_base64_seed_to_text.rs` — one-shot migration
  tool used to rewrite the historical base64-chunked bundle into the
  new plain-text layout. Kept for reproducibility.
- **Deleted**: `data/seed/translations.lino`,
  `src/translation/dictionary.rs`, `examples/build_translation_dictionary.rs`.

### Semantic-identity HTTP cache
- `src/translation/cache.rs` keys URLs by **kind of data**, not URL
  hash: `data/wikidata-cache/{search,entities,query,sparql}/`,
  `data/wiktionary-cache/<lang>/`, `data/http-cache/misc/`. All three
  roots are gitignored — the on-disk cache is a local accelerator
  written by `FORMAL_AI_LIVE_API=1` runs; the committed source of
  truth is the seed bundle above.
- `CachedHttpClient::get` consults three layers in order: seed bundle
  → on-disk accelerator → live HTTP.

### Rust pipeline
- `src/translation/wikidata.rs` — SPARQL lexeme join now requires
  `wikibase:lexicalCategory` to match between source and target
  lexemes, so polysemous surfaces like `water` no longer cross
  noun ↔ verb boundaries (was `water → поливать`, now `water → вода`).
- `src/translation/pipeline.rs` — Stage 1a follows
  `{{see translation subpage|...}}` to the `/translations` subpage
  when the main page only carries the marker.
  `FORMAL_AI_TRANSLATION_DEBUG=1` enables verbose stage-by-stage
  tracing (closes the issue #218 future-work item).

### Browser worker
- `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js` — removed `TRANSLATION_DICTIONARY` and
  `lookupDictionary`. Added `liveWiktionaryTranslate(surface, source,
  target)` that calls
  `*.wiktionary.org/w/api.php?action=parse&page=...&prop=wikitext&format=json&origin=*`
  directly, follows `{{see translation subpage|...}}` to the
  `/translations` subpage, and extracts the `{{tt+|<lang>|...}}` /
  `{{t|...}}` template payload. `translateSurface` and `tryTranslation`
  are now async; the only caller already runs inside `async`.
- `src/web/seed_loader.js` — removed the `extractTranslations` parser
  and the `translations` field; the worker no longer hydrates an
  offline dictionary.
- Mobile-friendly by design: nothing pre-bundled, the MediaWiki action
  API is CORS-friendly through `origin=*`, the seed bundle keeps the
  worker payload tiny.

### Tests & docs
- `tests/unit/specification/translation_via_links.rs` — `issue_221_*`
  regressions covering quoted + unquoted common-noun translation in
  both directions.
- `tests/unit/data_files.rs` — verifies every shipped `.lino` file
  parses through `lino_objects_codec::format::parse_indented`, stays
  under 1500 lines, and never falls back to typed-object encoding.
- `tests/unit/docs_requirements.rs` — adds `data/seed/api-cache` to
  the `is_skipped_tree` allowlist so the deferred-labels scan does
  not trip on verbatim Wikipedia wikitext that legitimately contains
  forbidden substrings (`mvp`, `poc`, etc.).
- `tests/e2e/tests/issue-221.spec.js` — Playwright coverage of the
  live-worker path: quoted RU→EN, quoted EN→RU, unquoted prompts,
  Russian inflected forms via MediaWiki redirect, round-trip stability.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-221/{README.md,online-research.md}` —
  rewritten for the API-cache + live-worker architecture; preserves
  the original investigation timeline and adds the 2026-05-22 entries.
- `changelog.d/20260522_000500_issue_221_translation_common_nouns.md`
  — rewritten changelog fragment.

## Before / After

| Prompt | Before (v0.100.0) | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Переведи "помидор" на английский.` | `"[en] помидор"` | `"tomato"` |
| `Переведи "огурец" на английский.` | `"[en] огурец"` | `"cucumber"` |
| `переведи "картофель" на английский` | `"[en] картофель"` | `"potato"` |
| `translate "tomato" to russian` | `"[ru] tomato"` | `"помидор"` |
| `translate "carrot" to russian` | `"[ru] carrot"` | `"морковь"` |
| `translate "water" to russian` | `"поливать"` (verb!) | `"вода"` |
| `переведи помидор на английский` (unquoted) | `"[en] помидор"` | `"tomato"` |

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build --release` — clean.
- [x] `cargo clippy --release --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean.
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — clean.
- [x] `cargo test --release` — 229 lib + 11 integration + 462 unit
  tests pass, including the `issue_221_*` regressions, the existing
  issue #218 coverage, and the new `lino_data_files_are_parseable_…`
  parser-roundtrip check on the regenerated seed bundle.
- [x] `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` — all shipped files
  inside their configured caps.
- [x] `FORMAL_AI_LIVE_API=1 cargo run --release --example refresh_translation_cache`
  — every seeded entry resolved; bundle re-written under
  `data/seed/api-cache/` within the 128-record / 1500-line caps.
- [ ] CI green on `windows-latest`, `macos-latest`, `ubuntu-latest`,
  plus Lint, Format, Coverage, and E2E (waiting on this push).
- [ ] `npm --prefix tests/e2e run test:local -- tests/issue-221.spec.js`
  — exercises the live Wiktionary fallback in the browser worker
  (requires network access during the run).

Fixes #221


## CI follow-ups (2026-05-22, after re-run)

Four small fixes layered on top of the seed-bundle rewrite so the
pipeline survives the live-API-first browser path on CI's network and
honours the reviewer's "must be human-readable" requirement:

- **`build.rs` — preserve chunk order across split records.** The
  lexicographic `files.sort()` placed `wiktionary-pages-part10.lino`
  before `-part2.lino` and the bare `wiktionary-pages.lino` (chunk 0)
  after every part, so any record split into multiple `.lino` files
  had its chunks reassembled out of order. Sort by
  `(bucket, part_index)` instead. Only split records were affected
  (apple failed; tomato fit in a single chunk).
- **`extractWiktionaryTranslation` — handle Russian Wiktionary
  blocks.** Russian Wiktionary uses
  `{{перев-блок|<sense>\n|<lang>=[[surface]]\n|...}}` rather than the
  English `{{t|<lang>|...}}` template family. The worker now matches
  both. Also added an NFD-based combining-mark strip so English
  Wiktionary's `{{t+|ru|помидо́р}}` (with U+0301 acute) resolves to the
  expected `помидор`.
- **`liveWiktionaryTranslate` — parallelize direct fetch and lemma
  resolution.** Inflected forms like `помидоры` need a 404, an
  OpenSearch round-trip to the lemma `помидор`, and a fetch of that
  lemma page. Running the first fetch and the OpenSearch lookup via
  `Promise.all` saves one sequential round-trip and keeps the assertion
  under Playwright's 5 s `expect` cap in CI.
- **`data/seed/api-cache/*.lino` — store responses as plain text.**
  Reviewer feedback: "We should not encode raw API data in .lino files
  as base64, it should be all human readable." Cache reader/writer
  now use Links Notation's doubled-quote escape (`""` represents one
  literal `"`) instead of base64; chunker never starts a chunk with
  `"` so split points always land on the safe side of an LN delimiter
  run. Wiktionary bucket shrinks from 18 to 4 parts (raw text is ~⅓
  the size of its base64 form).

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/221

Adds Wiktionary/Wikidata polysemy overrides so noun↔verb homographs like
`milk → молоко` (not `доить`), `water → вода` (not `поливать`), and
`fish → рыба` (not `рыбачить`) resolve to the canonical noun. Wires the
override into `TranslationPipeline::translate` and gates a new
`FORMAL_AI_TRANSLATION_DEBUG=1` stderr trace through every stage so
cache-miss, sparse-Wiktionary-table, and lexeme-polysemy paths can be
distinguished in a single run.

Adds three regression tests (`issue_221_*`) covering quoted/unquoted
common-noun translation in both directions. Updates the documentation
requirements test to skip `data/translation-cache/` since raw Wiktionary
bodies contain ISO 639-3 language codes that collide with reserved
labels.

commits.messageBody:
…s (issue #221)

The previous revision of this PR shipped 3,400+ tiny `.body` / `.url` files
under `data/translation-cache/`, plus an 826-line `translation-dictionary.json`,
which made the diff unreviewable and locked the cache to one consumer.

This commit reshapes the offline translation strategy:

- Drop `data/translation-cache/` (URL-hash cache) and `src/web/translation-dictionary.json`.
- Add `data/seed/translations.lino` — a single reviewable 128-entry Links
  Notation file shared between the Rust pipeline (embedded via `include_str!`)
  and the browser worker (fetched through the standard seed loader). It
  stays well under the 1,500-line per-file cap.
- Add `src/translation/dictionary.rs` to parse the .lino file into a
  bidirectional `(language, surface) → entry` map plus a reverse
  `(target_lang, target_surface) → entry` map so a single en→ru entry
  covers both directions and the cap stays meaningful.
- Reorganise `src/translation/cache.rs` to route responses by semantic
  identity: `wikidata-cache/{search,entities,query,sparql}/`,
  `wiktionary-cache/<lang>/`, `http-cache/misc/`. All three roots are
  gitignored — the cache is a local accelerator, not pre-seeded data.
- Wire the translation pipeline to consult the dictionary as stage 0;
  HTTP stages still run when the dictionary misses.
- Rewrite `examples/build_translation_dictionary.rs` to emit the .lino
  artefact, with deterministic Russian/English inflection generators
  feeding the `aliases` lines.
- Update the browser worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) and seed
  loader (`src/web/seed_loader.js`) to consume the .lino file directly
  instead of the bespoke JSON.
- Refresh the case study (`docs/case-studies/issue-221/`) and changelog
  fragment to describe the new layout.
commits.messageHeadline: Narrow translation cache to 128-noun .lino + semantic-identity bucket…

commits.messageBody:
`translation::cache::tests::cache_paths_use_semantic_subdirectories`
asserted with `contains("http-cache/misc/")`, which only matches on
Unix. Windows renders the same path as `…\http-cache\misc\…`, so the
assertion failed on `windows-latest` even though the layout was
correct. Walk the path's components and look for the two segments
side-by-side instead.
commits.messageHeadline: Make cache subdir assertion path-separator-agnostic (Windows CI)

commits.messageBody:
The test `cache_paths_use_semantic_subdirectories` failed on Windows
because `PathBuf::from("/tmp/whatever")` mixed with backslash-joined
subdirectories produced a path whose `components()` iteration didn't
yield the expected adjacent `["http-cache", "misc"]` pair.

Switch to a single-component relative cache root and assert the
semantic mapping directly via `cache_location`, which returns
relative components and is platform-agnostic.
commits.messageHeadline: Fix cache_paths test to work on Windows (issue #221)

commits.messageBody:
PR #222 review rejected the offline translation dictionary: the cache
must only store verbatim Wikipedia/Wikidata/Wiktionary API responses,
and the formalization pipeline must replay them through the
source → meaning → target path. This commit:

- Deletes data/seed/translations.lino, examples/build_translation_dictionary.rs,
  and src/translation/dictionary.rs.
- Adds data/seed/api-cache/*.lino — verbatim API response bodies in
  indented Links Notation, base64 payloads (RFC 4648, 76-char chunks),
  capped at 128 records per bucket and ≤1500 lines per file. Oversize
  bodies split into <bucket>-partN.lino parts and rejoin at load by URL.
- Adds build.rs to enumerate the .lino files at compile time and emit
  seed_bundle_files.rs into OUT_DIR; cache.rs picks it up via include!.
- Reorganises src/translation/cache.rs by semantic identity
  (wikidata-cache/{search,entities,query,sparql}, wiktionary-cache/<lang>,
  http-cache/misc) and keeps the on-disk accelerator gitignored.
- Removes the TRANSLATION_DICTIONARY plumbing and extractTranslations
  parser from the browser worker; adds liveWiktionaryTranslate that
  hits *.wiktionary.org/w/api.php with origin=* (CORS-friendly) and
  follows {{see translation subpage|...}} to /translations when present.
- Adds wikibase:lexicalCategory filter to the lexeme-join SPARQL so
  polysemous surfaces like "water" no longer cross noun↔verb boundaries.
- Updates examples/refresh_translation_cache.rs to drive the full
  pipeline, populate the on-disk accelerator, and rebundle into the
  committed .lino seed files (splitting oversize records, cleaning
  stale parts).
- Updates tests/e2e/tests/issue-221.spec.js for the live-worker path.
- Rewrites changelog fragment and docs/case-studies/issue-221/ to
  reflect the API-cache + live-worker architecture.

cargo build, cargo test (461 passed), cargo clippy --all-targets,
cargo fmt --check all clean.
commits.messageHeadline: Replace pre-seeded dictionary with raw API-response cache (issue #221)

commits.messageBody:
Two CI failures on the previous HEAD traced to two distinct bugs:

* `build.rs` enumerated seed `.lino` files with lexicographic sort, so
  `wiktionary-pages-part10.lino` loaded before `-part2.lino` and the
  bare `wiktionary-pages.lino` (chunk 0) loaded after every part. The
  base64-chunked bodies for split records were reassembled out of
  order, producing corrupt JSON only for entries large enough to span
  multiple parts (apple failed, tomato fit in one chunk and passed).
  Sort by `(bucket, part_index)` so chunk 0 → 1 → 2 → ... → N.

* The browser worker's `extractWiktionaryTranslation` only matched
  English Wiktionary `{{t|<lang>|...}}` style templates. Russian
  Wiktionary uses `{{перев-блок|sense\n|<lang>=[[surface]]\n|...}}`,
  so Russian-source lookups returned the fallback `[en] помидор`.
  English Wiktionary also stores Russian entries with the combining
  acute U+0301 (помидо́р), which never matched the expected `помидор`.
  Add a Russian-block pattern and an NFD-based combining-mark strip.

Verified locally with on-disk caches moved aside so seeds are the only
source: `cargo test --release` passes all 462 tests.
commits.messageHeadline: Fix seed bundle chunk order and Russian Wiktionary parsing (issue #221)

commits.messageBody:
For inflected forms like `помидоры`, the source-language Wiktionary
page does not exist as a standalone entry — the dictionary lemma lives
at `помидор`. The previous chain was three sequential round-trips:

  1. fetch(`помидоры`) → 404
  2. opensearch(`помидоры`) → `помидор`
  3. fetch(`помидор`) → wikitext

In CI, that exceeded the 5 s Playwright expect timeout. Issue (1) and
(2) the calls in parallel via `Promise.all`, so the overall cost in
the inflected-form path drops from three sequential round-trips to two.
The lemma path is unaffected: the speculative opensearch result is
unused when the direct fetch succeeds.
commits.messageHeadline: Parallelize Wiktionary direct fetch and lemma resolution (issue #221)

commits.messageBody:
PR feedback (2026-05-22T13:06:01Z): "We should not encode raw API data
in .lino files as base64, it should be all human readable."

* `cache.rs`: drop the base64 chunked storage. `body` chunks now hold
  the verbatim response text and the parser concatenates them as-is.
  Quotes inside chunks use Links Notation's doubled-quote escape
  (`""` represents a single literal `"`); no other character is
  escaped. The chunker never starts a chunk with `"`, so split points
  always land on the safe side of an LN delimiter run.

* `refresh_translation_cache.rs`: same change on the writer side.
  Drops the `base64_encode` / `split_into_chunks` plumbing and emits
  `body` chunks via `escape_lino_string` + `split_body_into_chunks`.
  Updates each bucket's `seed_metadata` header to describe the new
  format.

* `experiments/convert_base64_seed_to_text.rs`: one-shot migration
  tool that reads the existing base64-chunked `.lino` files,
  decodes them, and rewrites the same buckets in the new format.
  Kept in `experiments/` per the contributor guidelines so the
  migration is reproducible.

* `tests/unit/docs_requirements.rs`: add `data/seed/api-cache` to the
  `is_skipped_tree` allowlist. Verbatim Wikipedia/Wiktionary wikitext
  legitimately contains substrings the deferred-labels test forbids
  (e.g. `mvp` appearing inside Wikipedia transliteration text), so we
  exclude the raw seed cache from that scan — these are not
  source-code deferral labels.

* `data/seed/api-cache/*.lino`: regenerated via the converter so the
  shipped seed is now plain readable JSON instead of base64. The
  Wiktionary bucket shrinks from 18 to 4 parts because the raw text
  is roughly 1/3 the size of its base64 encoding.

Validated: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets
--all-features`, `cargo test --release` (229 lib + 11 integration +
